page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6v-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance belonging to the ecs.gn6v family. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes a single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. It does not include local NVMe storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the CPU and memory subsystems operate in the lower tiers compared to modern hardware. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, along with Geekbench scores, fall into the bottom 25% or bottom 10% categories. However, the system maintains average performance in large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations, as well as Passmark CPU encryption tests. The lack of AVX-512 and AMX extensions limits its performance in specialized vector and matrix math operations on the CPU.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance provides an entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads that require dedicated host cores and Volta-generation hardware without the overhead of newer, more expensive GPU architectures. It is best suited for workloads that leverage GPU acceleration for parallel processing, such as legacy deep learning training, GPU-accelerated database queries, and moderate rendering tasks, while having modest CPU performance requirements.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6v-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of system memory and a single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage and operates with a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows that while CPU and single-threaded performance are positioned in the lower percentiles, large-block memory bandwidth and encryption tasks perform at average levels. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for legacy GPU-accelerated workloads, machine learning inference, and parallel processing tasks that do not demand high-performance host CPU capabilities or local NVMe storage.
